info.title: microVM Configuration Schema
info.description: Infrastructure-as-code definition for a microVM on Unraid. The VMM (cloud-hypervisor or firecracker) is determined by the config filename, not a field in the schema.

VMConfig.description: A microVM configuration stored as cloud-hypervisor.json or firecracker.json in the VM directory.

name.description: VM identifier. Lowercase alphanumeric and hyphens only.

vcpus.description: Number of virtual CPUs allocated to the VM.

autostart.description: Start VM automatically when the Unraid array starts.

type.description: Storage backend type. 'thin' uses device-mapper thin provisioning (space-efficient, copy-on-write). 'raw' uses a raw ext4 file on disk.

size_mb.description: Virtual disk size in MB. For thin type, actual usage grows on write.

thin_device_id.description: Device-mapper thin device ID. Auto-assigned on VM creation. Required when type is 'thin'.

Network.description: Network configuration. VMs get a static IP on the host bridge.

ip.description: Static IPv4 address assigned to the VM.

mac.description: MAC address for the VM's virtual NIC. Auto-generated if not specified at creation.

bridge.description: Host bridge interface the TAP device is attached to.

tap_id.description: Numeric TAP interface ID. Creates tap{N} on the host and attaches it to the bridge.

source.description: Image source type. 'oci' pulls and extracts an OCI/Docker container image. 'existing' uses a pre-existing rootfs on disk.

ref.description: OCI image reference (required when source is 'oci'). Standard Docker image notation.

Kernel.description: Kernel boot configuration. The kernel binary path is derived at runtime from the VMM type (filename).

cmdline.description: Kernel command line arguments. Network IP parameters are appended automatically at boot time from the network config.
